summary: This is an extremely brief post where Joel Spolsky links to an article from The Onion, a satirical news publication, suggesting it makes a point he had previously attempted to make, but more effectively. The post contains no substantive argument of its own beyond the link and a brief attribution. Without access to the linked Onion article or knowledge of which prior post Joel is referencing, the full context is lost. It appears to be a quick link-blog entry common in early 2000s blogging.
key_points: Joel references a prior argument he made and credits The Onion with expressing it better
The linked Onion article appears to be about a Burger King employee, likely making a satirical point about work or management
This is a micro-post typical of early blogging culture, serving as a curated link rather than original analysis
money_quotes: The Onion says it better than I did
key_insight: Sometimes the best commentary is simply pointing to someone else who said it better.
